name: Hoosier Harvest Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Peeled, sliced tart baking apples (about 2 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. For the crust: In a bowl, combine flour, granulated sugar, and salt. Cut in butter-flavored shortening until the mixture forms pea-sized chunks. Add water, egg, and vinegar; mix lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide into two halves, press between hands to form two 5- to 6-inch pancakes. Wrap in plastic or aluminum foil and refrigerate.
3. Roll out one half of the dough for the bottom crust into a circle, trimming 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch pie plate. Loosen the dough carefully, fold into quarters, unfold, press into the pie plate, and trim even with the edge of the pie plate.
4. For the filling: In a bowl, combine brown sugar, granulated sugar, flour, and cinnamon. Cut in butter-flavored shortening until crumbs form. Toss apples with the crumb mixture and pour into the unbaked pie shell. Sprinkle cider over the filling.
5. Roll out the remaining dough for the top crust, using a cookie cutter to make a cut-out in the center. Remove the cut-out, sprinkle lightly with sugar and cinnamon, and place on top of the pie. Press edges together. Make small vents in the top crust for steam escape; use additional pastry pieces to create more vents.
6. Brush the rim with water, then gently press the extra pastry pieces into place around the edges.
7. Bake at 375°F for 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown. Cover the edge of the pie with foil during the last 10 minutes if necessary to prevent over-browning. Cool until just warm before serving. Makes one 9-inch pie.
